title: Buttery Crumbly British Pork Pie
description: A traditional British dish made with minced pork, buttery crust, and a crumbly texture, perfect for meal prep and diabetic-friendly diets.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the minced pork, salt, black pepper, dried sage, and onion powder. Mix well.
In a separate bowl, combine the flour and cold unsalted butter. Use a pastry blender or fingers to work the butter into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the ice-cold water to the flour mixture, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead a few times until it becomes smooth.
Divide the dough in half and roll out each half into a circle, about 3mm thick.
Spoon the pork mixture into the center of one of the dough circles, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Brush the edges of the dough with the beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Place the second dough circle on top of the filling and press the edges to seal.
Use a knife to create a decorative edge and cut a few slits in the top crust to allow steam to escape.
Place the pie on a baking sheet and bake for 45-50 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.
